title: schnorr48: redundant length check on s_bytes is dead code
description:
File: python/src/lichen/crypto/schnorr48.py
Lines: 134-135

The check `if len(s_bytes) != 32: return False` is dead code:

```python
if len(pubkey) != 32 or len(sig) != 48:  # Line 126-127
    return False
...
s_bytes = sig[16:48]  # Line 134
if len(s_bytes) != 32:  # Line 135 - ALWAYS FALSE
    return False
```

Since len(sig) == 48 is already verified on line 126-127, sig[16:48] will always be exactly 32 bytes. The check on line 135 can never be True.

Impact: Minor dead code, no functional bug. Remove for clarity.
close_reason: Dead code removed (already addressed in prior refactor); tests pass; committed and pushed.
